message:
fix(engine): prevent signed integer overflow in arithmetic across 7 modules

Systematic fix for potential signed integer overflow panics (debug) or
wrapping (release) that could cause incorrect results or crashes:

- controller_interference_guard: replace `(a - b).abs()` with
  `a.abs_diff(b) as i64` for timescale separation calculation —
  subtraction of two unsigned-origin i64 values could overflow when
  the difference exceeds i64::MAX

- counterexample_synthesizer: same `abs_diff` fix for timescale
  difference computation in interference detection

- expected_loss_selector: three fixes in confidence interval and
  borderline sensitivity calculations:
  - Use `unsigned_abs()` instead of `.abs()` to avoid i64::MIN panic
  - Use `abs_diff` for runner-up margin computation
  - Use `unsigned_abs()` for threshold calculation

- operator_safety_copilot: use `unsigned_abs()` in `format_millionths`
  to prevent panic when formatting i64::MIN (which has no positive
  i64 representation)

- mmr_proof: fix `is_all_ones` to use bitwise trick `(n & n+1) == 0`
  instead of `n == (1 << count_ones) - 1` which overflows for large
  values; guard `peak_positions` shift operations against h==64 overflow

- causal_replay: verify chain_hash for empty traces (previously accepted
  any hash for empty trace records); fix counterfactual re-decision to
  correctly handle remapped duplicate actions (keep minimum cost) and
  fall back to original action when no alternative meets threshold

- conformance_harness: fix off-by-one in TOML quoted string parser —
  `len() <= 2` rejected the valid minimal quoted string `""` (len==2);
  corrected to `len() < 2`

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
